summary:
The young male likely has a pilonidal sinus causing his symptoms. Given the prevalence and efficacy reported, using the Limberg flap surgery appears appropriate initially until more specific factors suggest an alternative method may offer superior results considering each case individually. Key point here being adherence to standard practice balanced openness toward potential innovations when indicated clinically. 

The primary recommendation centers strongly on employing the Limberg flap procedure first but keeping flexibility regarding possible adjustments if new evidence emerges supporting another technique specifically addressing current nuances observed within this patient scenario. This maintains both consistency across similar cases alongside responsiveness should novel insights prove advantageous moving forward into ongoing care pathways designed holistically responsive
